title: The Fulcrum Project
subtitle: An ecosystem of conscious diagnosis and leverage for the age of AI — diagnosed with its own methodology.
opening: One Sunday in June, the co-authors of a book on conscious leverage decide to apply their own framework to their own project. What they find is precisely the pattern the book diagnoses as the most common: solid material, reasonable epistemic, insufficient relational. The theory proves itself by being applied to itself.

material.detail: Their own domain. Seven apps deployed. A book published with an ISBN. Intellectual Property registered. Wikidata. Infrastructure across three servers.
material.crack: Crack: incomplete discoverability. Knowledge Panel and Schema.org still pending.

epistemic.detail: An original framework of four fulcrums and seven layers. Validated by 12 AI models. Legally registered. A coherent, applicable methodology.
epistemic.crack: Crack: external credibility assumed. No reviews, citations, or earned media. A 90-day experiment underway.

relational.detail: The systemic bottleneck — consistent with the book's own finding. A trust network limited to the inner circle. No verified community beyond the first ring.
relational.crack: This is the gap the book would diagnose in any other project — and here it diagnoses it in itself.

provenance.detail: The strongest axis. Two years of documented collaboration. A constitutional document. Co-authorship declared within the book. The chain is not hidden — it is shown.
provenance.crack: The new aura practiced, not merely theorized. Transparency as a generative act.

lever: Seven apps, a book, diagnostic tools, technical infrastructure across three servers. All of it reproducible. Anyone with access to the same AI tools could build a similar ecosystem in weeks. The lever is a commodity.
fulcrum: The four-fulcrum methodology applied systematically. Co-authorship declared as proof of the thesis. The two-year provenance chain — documented, transparent, unbroken. What cannot be regenerated.

contrast.text: Compare with the art restorer (Card #021): provenance equally strong, but the restorer has all four axes verified. The difference lies in the relational — where the restorer has decades of institutional trust, this project is just beginning.

lesson: The most honest diagnosis of a framework is the one that reveals its own weakness. A theory proves itself not when it works on others — but when it works on you and shows you where you fail.
references: Vol. 1, Ch. 9 — The relational fulcrum and the sequence
Vol. 2, Ch. 23 — Provenance: the one thing that cannot be regenerated
